model_ensemble_weights.notes: Weights calibrated from historical back-testing accuracy on 192 matches (2014-2022). Ranking gap model weighted highest due to best individual accuracy (73%).

ranking_gap_model.notes: k=0.065 steepness factor, threshold=15 ranking positions. When gap = threshold, P = 50%. Calibrated against 2014-2022 upset incidence by rank gap bracket.

logistic_regression.formula: logit(P) = intercept + sum(factor_i * coefficient_i). P = 1 / (1 + e^(-logit))
logistic_regression.notes: 68% accuracy. Strongest predictors: defending_champion (0.8) and ranking_gap (0.06 per rank position).

rules.condition: defending champion is playing in group stage (not first match only, entire group stage)
rules.historical_support: 3 of 4 defending champions since 2010 struggled significantly in group stage (Italy 2010, Spain 2014, Germany 2018 all eliminated; France 2022 nearly lost to Tunisia)
rules.notes: Applies throughout defending champion's group stage campaign, not just opening match

rules.condition: AFC confederation team vs FIFA top-10 ranked opponent in group stage
rules.historical_support: 5 of 8 upsets in 2022 involved Asian teams (Japan x2, Saudi Arabia, South Korea, Australia). Japan beat both Germany and Spain.
rules.notes: Strongest effect for Japan and South Korea; weaker for lower-ranked Asian teams

rules.condition: match year >= 2022 AND the lower-ranked team has demonstrated squad depth (can field 4+ attacking subs)
rules.historical_support: 2022 had 57% more upsets than 2018 (11 vs 7). Japan's '田忌赛马' strategy exemplified this.
rules.notes: Requires depth assessment. Not all lower-ranked teams benefit equally from 5 subs.

rules.condition: another upset has already occurred in the same group in the current tournament
rules.historical_support: 80% of groups with one upset had at least one additional upset (e.g., Group E 2022: Germany-Japan then Japan-Spain; Group F 2018: Germany-Mexico then South Korea-Germany)
rules.notes: Psychological cascade effect. Once a group's 'invincibles' are shown vulnerable, all teams in the group become more competitive.

rules.condition: match is in the host continent AND the team has strong football culture roots (top-20 in football tradition, not just ranking)
rules.historical_support: European teams in Europe 2018 (all 4 semi-finalists from Europe). Brazil in South America 2014 (4th place). Russia as host 2018 (QF). Qatar 2022 as counterexample (no football culture).
rules.notes: Negative boost = reduces upset probability. Only applies for teams with established football culture.

rules.condition: match is knockout stage (round_of_16, quarter_final, semi_final, final, third_place)
rules.historical_support: Knockout upset rate is ~5% vs group stage rate of ~9%. Only 5 knockout upsets across 3 tournaments (22 total). Most knockout upsets decided by penalty shootouts.
rules.notes: Strong negative effect. Knockout matches heavily favor higher-ranked teams due to defensive tactics and experience.

rules.condition: match year >= 2018 AND stage is group AND the underdog plays a disciplined defensive style (few fouls, organized shape)
rules.historical_support: VAR era saw 29 penalties in 2018 vs 13 in 2014. Underdogs who defend cleanly benefit from VAR catching higher-ranked teams' infractions.
rules.notes: Smaller effect. VAR benefits underdogs who are defensively disciplined; hurts those who rely on physical/aggressive defending.

rule_based_engine.notes: 65% accuracy. Most interpretable model. Rules should be applied additively from base rate of 10.5%. Final probability clamped to [0.05, 0.95].

trend_extrapolation.notes: 2026 with 48 teams = 104 matches (vs 64 previously). More matches mechanically increases upset count. 18 projected upsets = ~17% upset rate (higher than 2022's ~11% rate).

high.condition: all 4 models agree within 10 percentage points AND strong historical precedent exists
high.description: High confidence: clear signal across all models

medium.condition: models diverge 10-20 percentage points OR limited historical precedent
medium.description: Medium confidence: directional signal but significant uncertainty

low.condition: models diverge >20 percentage points OR speculative matchup (e.g., teams from different eras)
low.description: Low confidence: high uncertainty, treat as exploratory

historical_comparables.methodology: For any new match prediction, the system identifies the most similar historical upset from 2014-2022 data based on: ranking gap (40%), stage (25%), confederation match (20%), and year (15%).
historical_comparables.notes: Similarity scoring used to provide contextual reference points for predictions.
